title: Kalarivathukkal Bhagavathy Temple
tagline: Kalarivathukkal Bhagavathy Temple — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: Kalarivathukkal Bhagavathy Temple, Bhadrakali Shrine located near Valapattanam river.
overview.description: Kalarivathukkal Bhagavathy Temple, Bhadrakali Shrine located near Valapattanam river. The deity of the shrine is the fierce form of Bhadrakali. Kalarivathukkal Bhagavathy is considered as the mother of the ancient martial art Kalarippayattu and hence the name. The shrine is administered by Malabar Devaswom Board and classified as Category A Temple of the board.

topPlaces.name: Talap
topPlaces.description: Talap is a suburb of Kannur on NH 66 in Kerala in southern India. Talap is the nearest town in Kannur district.

topPlaces.name: Azhikode and Azhikkal
topPlaces.description: Azhikode is a coastal town and suburb of Kannur city in Kannur district of Kerala, India. Azhikode town is divided into Azhikode North and Azhikode South.

topPlaces.name: Pappinisseri
topPlaces.description: Pappinisseri is a census town in Kannur district in the Indian state of Kerala. Pappinissery have consistently won the Swaraj Trophy for best Grama Panchayat from 2017-2018 to 2021.

topPlaces.name: Kattampally
topPlaces.description: Kattampally is a village located in the eastern part of Kannur district, Kerala on the Valapattanam River. The nearest towns are Puthiyatheru and Kambil.

topPlaces.name: Valapattanam
topPlaces.description: Valapattanam is a census town and a suburb of Kannur city in the Kannur district, located in the Indian state of Kerala. It is also the smallest panchayat in Kerala.

topPlaces.name: Kannur North
topPlaces.description: Kannur North is a suburb of Kannur city in the state of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Pallikunnu
topPlaces.description: Pallikkunnu is a small township in Kannur, Kerala state, in South India. It is in Kannur corporation, on the National Highway between Kannur and Taliparamba.

topPlaces.name: Chirakkal, Kannur
topPlaces.description: Chirakkal is a census town in Kannur district in the state of Kerala, India. It is a suburb of Kannur city, about 7 km away.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kalarivathukkal Bhagavathy Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kalarivathukkal Bhagavathy Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kalarivathukkal Bhagavathy Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Calicut International Airport, Kozhikode (109 km) and the nearest railway station is Mangaluru Central (118 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kalarivathukkal Bhagavathy Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kalarivathukkal Bhagavathy Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kalarivathukkal Bhagavathy Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Kalarivathukkal Bhagavathy Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kalarivathukkal Bhagavathy Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Talap, Azhikode and Azhikkal, Pappinisseri, Kattampally, Valapattanam.
